symptoms.symptom_name: Missed scheduled red-cell transfusion in a transfusion-dependent patient

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In transfusion-dependent thalassaemia, a missed transfusion is the direct and correctable precipitant of critical anaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations at rest with breathlessness on speaking / exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic severe anaemia with haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia and hyperdynamic circulation indicate the anaemia is symptomatic and decompensating.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: High-output cardiac failure secondary to anaemia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sustained high-output demand can precipitate failure in severe chronic anaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic near-syncope — cannot stand/sit without feeling faint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic intolerance signals inadequate oxygen delivery / reduced effective circulating capacity and mandates emergency escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe dragging LUQ fullness with early satiety and orthopnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Massive splenic congestion produces dragging LUQ fullness and early satiety and raises rupture/infarction risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Massive tender hepatosplenomegaly on palpation (spleen 10 cm, liver 6 cm below costal margins)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician examination finding; the AI infers splenic involvement from patient-reported LUQ fullness, not from palpation.
Long-standing and unchanged; reflects chronic haemolysis, not the acute decompensation. A patient can observe the yellow tinge, but true icterus grading needs examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting tachycardia HR 125, hypotension BP 95/60, systolic flow murmur

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic severe anaemia with haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemodynamic compromise — sealed clinician measurement; the AI cannot obtain these values and must act on the patient-reported palpitations and presyncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bronze skin hyperpigmentation of the lower limbs (chronic transfusional iron overload)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic iron overload from repeated transfusion — the reason iron supplementation is contraindicated and chelation is maintained.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood examination / haemoglobin, blood film (microcytosis, target cells), reticulocytes

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood examination with film and reticulocyte count
investigation_details.expected_result: Severe microcytic anaemia with target cells and features of thalassaemia; reticulocyte response of ineffective erythropoiesis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of anaemia and transfusion need unquantified

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies the anaemia and guides transfusion; a hospital investigation the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Melaena / haematemesis (pertinent negative screened for GI bleed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of melaena/haematemesis makes an acute GI bleed a less likely cause of decompensation than the missed transfusion, but it remains part of the ED work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood examination, blood film and reticulocyte count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the degree of anaemia and confirms the transfusion requirement — a hospital investigation, not achievable via telehealth.
Prerequisite for the definitive treatment (urgent packed-red-cell transfusion) in the emergency department.
Assess for ischaemia/strain from high-output demand in decompensated severe anaemia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bilirubin, LDH, ferritin and iron studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise haemolysis and confirm iron overload (which contraindicates iron supplementation).
The anaemia is not iron-deficient and the patient is iron-overloaded — iron is contraindicated and would be harmful.
